fr_medoc_chateau_greysac_cru_bourgeois_s.appearance: Medium ruby-purple core with ruby rim; moderate depth of colour; clear; medium viscosity with slow-forming legs indicating moderate alcohol (14.5%)
fr_medoc_chateau_greysac_cru_bourgeois_s.nose_summary: Floral character with plum, berry and cassis aromas; tobacco leaf and baking spice notes; underlying earthiness with hints of dried cherry, cedar and warm gravel; some barnyard nuance with clean mineral undertone; moderate intensity
fr_medoc_chateau_greysac_cru_bourgeois_s.palate_summary: Soft entry with plum and cherry fruit, cassis and currant flavours; underlying earthiness and cocoa; tobacco leaf accent present mid-palate; gentle complexity rather than power; round red and dark fruit profile with subtle spice
fr_medoc_chateau_greysac_cru_bourgeois_s.structural_summary: Medium-bodied; medium, polished tannins that are fine-grained and unassertive; moderate acidity providing freshness; 14.5% abv; medium finish with gentle cassis persistence; approachable and forward in style; not built for extended ageing but holds well for 10-15 years in good vintages
sources: James Suckling (90 pts, 2018 vintage)
The Wine Cellar Insider (88 pts, 2018 vintage)
IntoWine.com (multiple vintage reviews)
Independence Wine & Spirits (2015 Cru Bourgeois tasting)
Palm Bay Wines (importer tech sheet, 2019 vintage)

us_napa_heitz_cellar_napa_valley_caberne.appearance: Medium to deep ruby-garnet with clear rim; bright and vivid colour; good clarity; moderate viscosity consistent with restrained 13.8% abv
us_napa_heitz_cellar_napa_valley_caberne.nose_summary: Red cherry, currant and cranberry dominant with dark raspberry and spiced plum; savory edge with dried herbs including sage and bay leaf; hints of cassis, juniper and floral notes; subtle chalk and dust; restrained and more red-fruited than dark-fruited for a Napa Cabernet; some cedar and tobacco with age; touch of menthol and mint
us_napa_heitz_cellar_napa_valley_caberne.palate_summary: Fresh and lively with red cherry, currant and plum flavours; boysenberry and dried herb notes; chalk and mineral undertones; savory and bright rather than jammy; focused and nuanced with lovely energy; hints of dark chocolate and mint on the finish; classic Napa expression without overripeness
us_napa_heitz_cellar_napa_valley_caberne.structural_summary: Medium to full-bodied but lighter-styled for Napa Valley; fine-grained tannins that are well-integrated and lightly drying; bright acidity providing freshness and lift; 13.8% abv (notably low for modern Napa); medium-plus to long finish with light red-fruited tartness; two years in French oak barrels (combination large and small) plus one year bottle age before release
sources: Antonio Galloni/Vinous (93 pts, 2018 vintage)
Decanter (94 pts, 2018 vintage)
Wine Enthusiast (92 pts, 2018 vintage)
The Napa Wine Project (detailed 2021 and 2015 tasting notes)
Wine Spectator (2021 review: full-bodied, textural, rounded mouthfeel)

au_coonawarra_wynns_black_label_cabernet.appearance: Deep ruby to opaque purple core with vibrant plum hue; excellent depth of colour; clear and bright; moderate viscosity
au_coonawarra_wynns_black_label_cabernet.nose_summary: Classic Coonawarra nose of black fruits with hint of mint and eucalyptus; blackberry, black cherry, cassis and dark cherry aromas; lavender and violet notes; savouriness with dried herbs, aniseed and white sage; subtle chocolate and black jellybean characters; oak embrace that adds rather than intrudes; slight dusty, chalky quality
au_coonawarra_wynns_black_label_cabernet.palate_summary: Dark cherry and blackberry pastille flavours with cassis of Coonawarra cabernet; seductive cherry and red berry augmenting the darker fruit; high energy and brightness driving to an effortlessly long finish; fine cedary oak; notes of moss and earth providing contrast; thyme on the finish; highly polished and elegant with suave poise
au_coonawarra_wynns_black_label_cabernet.structural_summary: Medium-bodied (described consistently as medium-weight); very fine, elegant tannins that are chalky-textured and pliable; bright acidity assisting length and lingering beautifully; 13.5-14% abv; long to very long finish; pristine varietal cabernet fruit balanced for cellaring (20+ years potential); matured 14 months in French oak hogsheads and barriques (21-31% new)
sources: Wynns Coonawarra Estate (official 2018 and 2023 tasting notes)
Winepilot.com (2019 vintage detailed review)
Natalie MacLean (96 pts, 2021 vintage)
Halliday Wine Companion / Tastingbook (2023 vintage, 94 pts)
Wine-pages.com (multi-vintage vertical tasting 2006-2015)

de_mosel_dr_loosen_wehlener_sonnenuhr_ka.appearance: Pale straw to light lemon-green; brilliant clarity with green-gold tints; light viscosity with fine, quick legs consistent with low alcohol (8% abv); possible micro-bubbles from residual CO2
de_mosel_dr_loosen_wehlener_sonnenuhr_ka.nose_summary: Delicate and refined with white peach, lemon zest and Meyer lemon tea aromas; lavender and honeydew melon; wet stone and slate minerality; herbal and spicy notes including hibiscus, blood orange and rosemary in some vintages; hints of passion fruit and mandarin orange; also showing green apple (Granny Smith) and yellow plum; shy but elegant
de_mosel_dr_loosen_wehlener_sonnenuhr_ka.palate_summary: Lively and dancing on the palate with white peach, lemon zest and ginger; passion fruit, saline and quince notes; pineapple and citrus nuances; beautifully light and vividly citric; delicately sweet with classic Mosel balance of fruit and racy acidity; pure peach and lemon fruit lifted by crisp blue-slate minerality
de_mosel_dr_loosen_wehlener_sonnenuhr_ka.structural_summary: Light-bodied; high, racy acidity from blue Devonian slate soils; delicately off-dry with ~47 g/L RS balanced by ~9.6 g/L TA; 8% abv; medium-plus finish that is delicately sweet with sustained acidity; brisk and mouthwatering mouthfeel; quintessential Mosel Kabinett elegance; ungrafted vines averaging 60 years old
sources: James Suckling (94 pts, 2022 vintage)
Wine Spectator (90 pts, 2023 vintage)
Wine Enthusiast (2022 review: cooked apple, white currant, pineapple)
Ken's Wine Guide (91 pts, 2022 vintage, detailed tasting)
Dr. Loosen / Tastingbook (producer description and vintage notes)

de_mosel_jj_prum_wehlener_sonnenuhr_spat.appearance: Medium lemon-yellow with obvious greenish tint; brilliant clarity; vibrant depth of colour from late-harvest concentration; light viscosity consistent with low alcohol (8% abv); notable brightness and vibrancy
de_mosel_jj_prum_wehlener_sonnenuhr_spat.nose_summary: Pure, clear and flinty; concentrated fresh fruit salad quality served on a slate plate; apricot and white peach with officinal herbs (marjoram dominant); smoky, reductive 'sponti' character from indigenous yeast fermentation that evolves with bottle age; honey and apricot in older examples; wildness that develops into extraordinary complexity; silky succulence
de_mosel_jj_prum_wehlener_sonnenuhr_spat.palate_summary: Super-delicate and precise despite considerable concentration; savory, clear and elegant with great precision and finesse; compact and tasty with perfect astringency and great minerality; lush and highly finessed but seriously structured; barely medium-bodied yet enormously long finish where natural grape sweetness is barely felt; citrusy persistence; stimulating salinity and mouth-watering grip
de_mosel_jj_prum_wehlener_sonnenuhr_spat.structural_summary: Light to barely medium-bodied; high, penetrating acidity providing serious structure and stimulating salinity; off-dry to medium-sweet (Spatlese level RS) but perceived as barely sweet due to immense acidity; 8% abv; enormously long finish; aged in traditional large-format old oak Fuder casks on lees; 70% ungrafted vines averaging 60 years, some exceeding 100 years; legendary ageability (drinking windows extending 25+ years)
sources: Wine Advocate (95+ pts, 2020 vintage: 'pure, clear and flinty')
James Suckling (96 pts, 2020 vintage: 'beams you up to Cloud 9')
Tastingbook (99 Tb points, 2019 vintage: 'apricot, white peach, marjoram')
WineBerserkers forum (2019 range tasting notes from Katharina Prum event)
YouTube wine review (2016 magnum: detailed colour, nose, palate assessment)

de_rheingau_robert_weil_kiedrich_grafenb.appearance: Medium straw-gold; brilliant clarity; moderate viscosity reflecting fuller body and higher alcohol for a dry German Riesling; slight green-gold tints
de_rheingau_robert_weil_kiedrich_grafenb.nose_summary: Complex and enticing with ripe apple, juicy pear and citrus fruits; nectarine, quince and elderflower; tangerine and yellow plum with citrus peel; honey and green apple; white peach, pomelo and touch of petrol; dense and reductive with sesame and corn flake notes in youth; fine mineral notes of slate and flint; subtle wild hops
de_rheingau_robert_weil_kiedrich_grafenb.palate_summary: Juicy and almost bouncy with Reine Claude (greengage) vividness; zesty ripe lemon resting on gentle yeast layer; concentrated with glimpses of tropical fruit within a frame of citric freshness; lime juice and green apple on the palate; cool and dynamic; vivid structure with brilliant citrus and orchard fruit; wet stone and saline nuance; creamy acidity with savory length
de_rheingau_robert_weil_kiedrich_grafenb.structural_summary: Medium to full-bodied (described as a 'powerhouse' and 'muscular elegance'); vibrant, laser-focused acidity providing tension and linearity; bone-dry (Trocken); approximately 12.5-13% abv; very long, clean finish echoing lime zest, wet stone and mineral; aged 10-12 months on lees in large neutral oak Doppelstuckfasser (Stockinger casks); phyllite soils with loess and loam; vines up to 80 years old, many ungrafted; exceptional ageing potential
sources: Vinous / Anne Krebiehl MW (96 pts, 2022 vintage: detailed tasting)
Wine Enthusiast (2021 vintage: nectarine, quince, elderflower, wild hops)
Wine Advocate (97 pts, 2024 vintage)
CellarTracker community (2021 vintage: white fruit, cool, spicy, bright acidity)
Bibendum Wine Co / Decantalo (producer notes and multiple vintage reviews)

fr_alsace_trimbach_cuvee_frederic_emile.appearance: Pale to medium straw-gold when young, developing to deep gold with age; brilliant clarity; moderate viscosity reflecting medium to full body; green-gold tints in youth
fr_alsace_trimbach_cuvee_frederic_emile.nose_summary: Great aromatic complexity with citrus, white flowers and flinty mineral notes; green apple, pear, lime and orange zest; quince and honey developing with age; petrol (kerosene) note that dissipates; apricot, ripe pear; chalky, crushed stone and slight sea air quality; smoky and lemony; toasted bread and white truffle with significant age; vibrant mineral and racy
fr_alsace_trimbach_cuvee_frederic_emile.palate_summary: Dry with halogen brightness; gorgeous acidity bringing to life flavours from fresh lemon juice and honey to paraffin and nut skin; pineapple and tropical hints balanced by lingering almond notes; stony and crisp with penetrating quality; peach, apple and chalk; slightly viscous texture; compact, dense and youthful even after years; saline and tensioned with complex finish pairing ripe dense fruit with structural elements
fr_alsace_trimbach_cuvee_frederic_emile.structural_summary: Medium to full-bodied; high, chiselled acidity that is the defining structural feature; bone-dry (under 5 g/L RS in 2017; 2.4 g/L RS); 13-14% abv; very long to extraordinarily long finish; no MLF; vinified in stainless steel and old casks; grapes from Grand Crus Geisberg and Osterberg on marl-calcaro-sandstone soils; old vines 45-55+ years; released ~7 years post-vintage; ageing potential 20-30+ years; fine tannins reflecting limestone/marl terroir
sources: Robert Parker's Wine Advocate (95 pts, 2015 and 2019 vintages)
James Suckling (95 pts, 2017 vintage from magnum)
Vinous (95+ pts, 2017 vintage: green apple, pear, lime, mineral)
Wine Spectator (2017 vintage: spiced apple, pear jelly, quince, chalky mineral)
IntoWine.com (2001 and 2011 vintage reviews: peach, minerality, long finish)

au_clare_valley_grosset_polish_hill_ries.appearance: Pale lemon-green to pale straw when young; brilliant clarity; light viscosity; developing to deeper lemon and eventually old gold with extended age; characteristically pale and bright in youth
au_clare_valley_grosset_polish_hill_ries.nose_summary: Vivid lime and kaffir lime with lemon blossom; green apple and crushed slate minerality; slightly waxy and chalky notes overlaying the citrus; pungent yet restrained; grapefruit, gingery apple juice and jasmine tea aromas; white pepper and talc-chalk; bath salts and gunmetal character; spearmint and merest petrol note with age; lemongrass and subtle herbs
au_clare_valley_grosset_polish_hill_ries.palate_summary: Bone-dry and steely with intense lime and lemon driving the palate; grapefruit and quince emerging mid-palate; crystalline mineral quality with great intensity; piercing acid line with chalky, puckering texture; pink grapefruit and talc-chalk minerality; concentrated, fresh and bright; fleshy texture from small berries (unfined and unfiltered); pinpoint focus and deft poise
au_clare_valley_grosset_polish_hill_ries.structural_summary: Light to medium-bodied; high, steely acidity from broken blue slate soils (500 million year old); bone-dry (RS 1.9 g/L); 12.1-12.9% abv; long to very long finish with cleansing, moreish aftertaste; stainless steel fermented; lees contact; certified organic and biodynamic; vines planted 1996 at 460-480m elevation on hard rocky alluvial loam over clay over blue slate; exceptional ageing potential (15-20+ years)
sources: Huon Hooke / The Real Review (96 pts, 2025 vintage)
Halliday Wine Companion / Mike Bennie (95 pts, 2025 vintage)
Campbell Mattinson / The Wine Front (96 pts, 2025 vintage)
James Suckling (2025 vintage: lemon blossoms, kaffir limes, crushed slate)
Wine-pages.com / CellarTracker (multi-vintage tasting: spearmint, petrol, poise)

au_eden_valley_pewsey_vale_estate_riesli.appearance: Pale straw with green hues; brilliant clarity; light viscosity consistent with 11.5% abv; bright lemon-straw colour; green tints characteristic of cool-climate, high-altitude Riesling
au_eden_valley_pewsey_vale_estate_riesli.nose_summary: Intense lime and citrus with white flowers and fine dried herbs; fresh lemon and lemon blossom; slight floral aspect with delicacy; minerally bath salts character; touch of petrol developing with age; stone fruit and peach undertone; lemon rind edginess; not lean despite citrus dominance, with a sense of ripeness and burgeoning fruit
au_eden_valley_pewsey_vale_estate_riesli.palate_summary: Dry with intense lime streaking through the palate; grapefruit and lemon with fresh rosemary and white pepper; green apple crunch; blossom character carrying through from nose providing filigree elegance; hint of tropical fruit; minerally feel as mouth-watering acidity carries to a long refreshing finish; rich and vivacious yet uncompromisingly intense; lime and acid-shaped
au_eden_valley_pewsey_vale_estate_riesli.structural_summary: Light to medium-bodied; high, fresh natural acidity balancing flavour intensity (TA 6.80 g/L, pH 3.1); dry (RS 1.20 g/L); 11.5% abv; medium-plus to long finish marked by bright acidity, tart citrus and fine length; wild yeast fermentation since 2011; single vineyard at 500m elevation in Eden Valley; winemaker Louisa Rose; Eden Valley's longer ripening period from cooler temperatures; medium to long-term cellaring potential (15-20 years)
sources: Pewsey Vale Vineyard (official 2025 tasting note)
Winebow / importer (2025 tech sheet and tasting notes)
MyWinePal (detailed multi-vintage comparison: 2019-2024)
OzWineReview (2024 vintage: steely, green-fruited, rocky acidity)
Wine-pages.com / Tom Cannavan (2020 vintage video review)
